title: Chapter Twenty-Three: Praying Mantis
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The guide was a local man named Jang-sam. He had the look of a household head supporting a family -- shabby hemp clothing, a loosely held money pouch, eyes smaller than even Yeon-shin's.

"Young master, is it really just you? Are you sure you're going to the Praying Mantis Sect?"

His eyes trembled.

Yeon-shin understood. The evil sects were different from even the black-path ruffians in the back alleys. Black-path groups would find and take revenge on informants. Evil sects had martial artists who could do far worse.

"I alone am sufficient."

"Th-that's truly admirable courage. But please reconsider. Dozens of them have learned martial arts. Even one of their small fry could sweep through the alleys of Lianhu."

"Courage? You seem to have studied."

"Just a country bumpkin who failed the civil service exam."

It was fortunate they could communicate. They had one night's travel to reach Lianhu County from Xi'an. Yeon-shin handed over a small salt pouch -- too precious for a commoner, not enough for a martial artist. The monthly salary from Desolate Sect was paid in silver coins, and he carried no copper.

Jang-sam's eyes widened as he looked inside.

"They came in three years ago," he said, his tongue loosening. "Pacified the streets of Lianhu. There aren't many places in Xi'an without a secular branch of the Zhongnan Sect, and Lianhu County was one of them. They've taken over a dozen people as servants. Their tyranny is severe, but the county magistrate is bought."

"Their martial arts?"

"All kinds of weapons. Swords, spears, strangely curved blades. I've seen them from afar -- so fast it's hard to follow with the eyes. With one sword strike, two heads flew off."

Quite strong. Even if the system of their martial veins was not established, they had learned enough to be called evil sect martial artists.

"The people they captured. What is the government office doing?"

"The county magistrate is in cahoots with them. There's no one to take care of it."

Yeon-shin briefly wondered whether a Desolate Sect martial artist would be allowed to behead an official.

---

After a night at a roadside inn, they reached Lianhu County.

A lively village came into view beyond the hill. At the end of the road, an exceptionally large and ornate pavilion. The signboard was visible even from here.

*Praying Mantis Sect.*

"You can go now," Yeon-shin said. "Thank you for the guidance."

He turned toward the main street. He felt someone following.

*Jang-sam. Perhaps his family was captured.*

The market street was quiet in the wrong way. Merchants bargained in low voices, their eyes sliding sideways. When Yeon-shin turned his head to where everyone was cautiously looking, the scene struck him still.

Women. Tied with ropes as if they were traitors.

Five martial artists wearing swords sat carelessly guarding them. Family members could only sob, unable to approach. Would their throats be cut if they made a sound?

"It's no wonder Desolate Sect was established," Yeon-shin said, deliberately loud, as he approached.

One of the guards threw a flying dagger without a word.

No force imbued. He caught it mid-flight, unfolded the Swift Sword Technique while relaxing the strength in his hand, and sent the dagger back.

It struck the thrower's forehead.

Corpse.

The four remaining guards got up, dusting off their seats.

"A youngster with some skill."

"The posture for throwing the dagger was strange. Did you learn it properly?"

Calm. Too calm for men who had just watched a comrade die. The attitude of men who had tasted all the pleasures of the world and grown bored with everything, including death.

Yeon-shin drew the Desolate Sword. The snow-white blade shattered the sunlight beneath his hand.

Their gazes changed.

"If we offer it to the sect leader--"

A flash of blue light. Blood spurted.

The man who had mentioned the sect leader knelt, blood-stained lips moving around a word that would never finish. Before the other three could turn, the snow-white sword path flashed three times.

Desolate Swift Sword Technique. The sensation of cutting transmitted through the sword wave. A fairly familiar feeling now.

He cut the ropes of the captive women. They thanked him with clasped hands but could not openly rejoice. Their eyes kept drifting toward the Praying Mantis Sect's pavilion.

*They're not just taking servants.*

He walked the road with his sword lowered, unsheathed. The street went completely quiet. People scattered. Old men clicked their tongues, muttering about some poor wandering swordsman who was going to die.

Only Jang-sam followed behind.

*Group battle.*

He had been thinking of solo missions since slaying the Blood Swordmaster. There was a limit to the credit gained by moving with the masters of Radiant Demon Wing. To walk the martial world alone, one had to be prepared to face dozens of swords. Short decisive battles with nemeses mostly existed in the romanticism of enthusiasts.

He kicked the main gate open.

Thirty martial artists were lined up in the training ground, waiting for the swordsman who had slain five of their sect's warriors.

The news had spread fast.

"That youngster?" The middle-aged man on the stairs frowned. He wore a long robe with all four divine beasts and dragons embroidered upon it.

*A rebel's robe. How detached martial artists are from the country.*

"Break his limbs and bring him." The sect leader paused. Then: "No. I'll do it personally."

He came down the stairs drawing his sword. A narrow-bladed weapon with waves of true energy pulsing along it. If Yeon-shin had been at the level of his Desolate Exam, he could not have won.

"What's your name?" Yeon-shin asked.

"Why would someone about to die need to hear my great name?"

"I need to know who the sect leader is to claim credit."

He aroused the energy of the Dynamic Technique. The moment he became one with the Desolate Sword through true energy, he felt the man's sword intent clearly.

The sect leader's thrust rode the wind -- a swift sword, fast and practised. But having created a fist technique that properly handled force, the path was visible.

Steel rang on steel. Yeon-shin struck upward, the layered true energy of the Fate Conquering Scripture in the blow. The sect leader's face dyed with perplexity as his blade was pushed aside.

"Hah!"

The surrounding disciples pounced. A sharp spear obscured the view. Flying daggers came. All kinds of blades from all directions.

His first time facing so many weapons at once.

The stinging wound from a wheel-blade at his lower back sharpened his senses. Expanded them.

Something shifted.

The lines created by all the blades were visible -- and the sword path that had to pass through them began to appear in his mind. As if another self was looking down from the sky, the movements of all four directions entered his awareness.

*Strike. Pass through. Strike and shift.*

The Desolate Sword, faster than anyone here, struck down weapons and induced collisions among them. A deflected flying dagger pierced another man's heart. A redirected spear went through the neck of the saber-wielder beside it.

Bodies fell. Blood rose.

The sect leader's narrow blade thrust through a gap in the chaos -- a different level from his men. Yeon-shin spun like a whirlwind and struck the flat of the blade.

The sect leader nearly dropped his sword and disappeared into the crowd of disciples.

"Amazing achievement! But your sword path is simple. I've already seen through your swift sword--"

Yeon-shin leapt over the remaining attacks like a leaf riding wind and appeared before the sect leader's face.

The true energy of the Eternal Blossom Fist climbed his arm. The second stance, Advancing Thunder, was possible even through the Desolate Sword now. The formless strength held in his hand. The true path of Radiant Demon rose from his feet to assist.

He covered the distance in a single breath.

The sect leader's eyes grew wide. Yeon-shin struck down from above his head.

The pure white sword light flashed.

A merciless sensation went through him like a shudder.

---
